  not before; a floor is guaranteed now (no sunset before 2027-04-12). Per-key
  extensions to a hard 2027-10-12 backstop; parity-gated v1-shape adapter.
  [checkable] T+60d gate: no account >20% of v1, no >9mo-notice contract, diffuse tail,
  adapter passes value-parity — else the date slips.
---

# ADR 0001 — Sunset of the v1 geocoding API

## Context
v1/v2 geocoding diverged enough that every bugfix is written and tested twice. v1 is a
thin, flat tail. Three customers (NorthRoute, Tavo, Ferrymark) oppose a sunset with
concrete blockers. The memo's decisive gap: per-customer migration cost is unknown.

Verified vs `version_traffic.csv` (verifier gate; reproduced by hand):
- [checkable] v1 = 0.40% (41,160 of 10,300,360). PASS.
- [contradiction] Memo's "last full month" is **14 days** (2026-05-16..29); 41k/10.3M are
  14-day totals, true monthly ~88k v1 / ~22M. Share unchanged; outreach sizes off ~88k.
- [checkable] v1 is **flat** (first-7 2,920/day vs last-7 2,960; no decay) → Option B has
  no terminus and a usage-floor never fires.

## Weakest assumption
Within 60 days we can enumerate/reach most of the tail, get real cost estimates, and prove
whether a value-parity-safe adapter is feasible. If not, the default date slips to backstop.

## Decision — Option A, amended
Commit the firm date **at T+60d after measuring cost, not before** (cost, not 0.40%, sizes
runway). Guarantee a minimum-notice floor now. Help and the global date are separate levers.

Discovery (T0→T+60d):
- **2026-07-12 (T0):** Deprecate v1; no new v1 keys. Floor: **sunset no earlier than
  2027-04-12 (≥9mo notice)**. No firm 410 date yet.
- **2026-07-26 (T+14d):** Instrument to enumerate **every** account (key, volume, contact).
  Emit RFC 8594 `Deprecation` header (in-band signal for dead inboxes).
- **2026-08-26 (T+45d):** Census, outreach, per-account cost estimates, **legal/SLA review**,
  and tech-lead **adapter feasibility + value-parity spike** all complete.
- **2026-09-10 (T+60d):** **Commit the date.** Default **2027-04-12** iff [checkable] gate
  passes (no account >20% of v1 without a plan; no >9mo-notice contract; diffuse tail; adapter
  parity holds); else set later, up to backstop. Publish RFC 8594 `Sunset` header.

Run-out (default 2027-04-12):
- **2027-01-12 (T-90d)** and **2027-03-12 (T-30d):** remaining-time notices.
- **2027-03-29 / 2027-04-05 (T-14d/T-7d):** pre-announced **brownouts** (brief v1 outages) to
  surface silent-but-live integrations recoverably, scheduled outside load-bearing windows.
- **2027-04-12:** firm sunset → **410 Gone**, except keys with an agreed extension.

Extension valve (per-key): hard backstop **2027-10-12 (T+15mo)**. Extend via an **agreed dated
plan** (assist co-authors it, so zero-bandwidth accounts qualify) — the bar is a committed date,
not proof of busyness; one slip allowed. A truthful plan needing >backstop escalates to a **paid
extended-support agreement** (human decision), not auto-410 and not auto-grant.

**No usage-floor and no zero-traffic auto-accelerator** (flat traffic: a floor never fires and an
aggregate trigger would irreversibly 410 a bursty quarterly/DR key). Calendar date is the sole trigger.

Three named accounts: immediate migration-assist; auto-eligible for extension via an agreed plan;
adapter offered **iff it passes value-parity for their traffic**; none can be surprise-410'd.
NorthRoute — adapter turns the parsing rewrite into an endpoint swap if parity holds, else extension.
Tavo — assist does the work; adapter likely near-zero. Ferrymark — adapter + plan; brownouts clear
of the nightly window.

Adapter: projects v2 output into v1's flat `lat/lng/label`. **Gated on a signed value-parity report**
(replay v1 vs v2+adapter, show divergence) — shape-identical is not value-identical; a drifted
coordinate in an unchanged payload is a confidently-wrong dispatcher pin. Parity fail → drop adapter,
give runway instead. Temporary projection, no geocoding logic, deleted at sunset.

## Rejected
B: flat traffic never stops → permanent dual-maintenance. C (60-day on 0.40%): volume ≠ dependency,
breaks warned-about workflows, sunsets before cost is known. Firm-date-day-one: commits runway before
the input that sets it.

## Red-team (12 breaks; folded/bounded, none dismissed)
1 window mislabel → size off census (~88k/mo). 2 date-before-cost → floor now, commit at T+60d (core
fix). 3 "no whale" unverified → [checkable] gate before commit. 4 valve excludes no-bandwidth accounts
→ bar is a committed date assist co-authors. 5 backstop guillotines truthful >15mo plan → paid support,
not auto-410. 6 adapter shape≠value → signed parity gate (sharpest). 7 zero-traffic accelerator →
**deleted**. 8 legal review late → moved pre-commit. 9 survivorship of stale integrations → in-band
headers + brownouts, and accept some break after good-faith notice. 10 hard 410 for embedded → brownouts
first. 11 self-attested plans → assist-agreed, backstop-capped, escalate on slippage. 12 adapter adds
maintenance → bounded, parity-gated, deleted at sunset, not shipped if parity fails.

## Revisit triggers
T+60d gate fails any [checkable] → reopen the date here before publishing. Census unlike "diffuse tail".
Named account's truthful plan needs >backstop → open a commercial ADR.
